seo.title: 20 Best Things To Do in Trat (2026 Guide)
seo.metaDescription: Discover the 20 best things to do in Trat and Koh Chang in 2026. Island hopping, jungle waterfalls, snorkeling, night markets, and durian tasting. Real prices and insider tips.

intro: Trat province is Thailand's island playground on the eastern seaboard — 52 islands, jungle waterfalls, underwater coral gardens, and one of the country's best night markets. Most visitors head straight to Koh Chang, but the outer islands of Koh Kood and Koh Mak offer unspoiled beauty that rivals anything on the Andaman coast. Back on the mainland, Trat Town hides a gem-trading heritage and Thailand's best durian. Here are 20 things that make this underrated province worth exploring.

activities.name: Beach Hop Along Koh Chang's West Coast
activities.description: Koh Chang's west coast has five distinct beaches connected by a single road. Start at White Sand Beach (Hat Sai Khao) in the north for the widest sand and most facilities, then work south through Klong Prao (longest and most peaceful), Kai Bae (village atmosphere), and end at Lonely Beach (Hat Tha Nam) for beach bars and backpacker energy. Each beach has a different personality — you can hit all four in a day by scooter.
tips: Rent a scooter to cover all beaches — songthaews are infrequent and expensive
Lonely Beach has the cheapest beer and best sunset bars
Kai Bae has a viewpoint overlooking the offshore islands

activities.name: Swim at Klong Plu Waterfall
activities.description: Koh Chang's most popular waterfall is a three-tiered cascade surrounded by dense jungle, with a deep natural swimming pool at the base. A well-maintained 500-meter trail through the national park leads to the falls. The cool mountain water is a welcome escape from the tropical heat, and the pool is large enough for proper swimming. Located inland from Klong Prao Beach.
activities.price: 200 THB entrance (~$6 USD) national park fee
tips: Visit before 10 AM to beat the tour groups
The same ticket works at Than Mayom Waterfall the same day
Wear water shoes — rocks are slippery around the pool
activities.bestTime: Year-round (best flow after rain in Jun-Oct)

activities.name: Island Hop to Koh Kood
activities.description: Take the Boonsiri catamaran from Koh Chang's Bang Bao pier or a speedboat from Laem Sok pier to Thailand's most unspoiled large island. Koh Kood has pristine white-sand beaches (Klong Chao, Ao Tapao), a jungle waterfall with swimming hole (Klong Chao Waterfall), and a fraction of Koh Chang's visitor numbers. The crystal-clear water rivals the Maldives. Stay at least two nights to truly unwind.
tips: Bring cash — no ATMs on the island
Most resorts close June-October
Boonsiri Ferry departs Bang Bao at 10:00, arrives Koh Kood 11:45

activities.name: Cycle Around Koh Mak
activities.description: Koh Mak is flat, car-free, and small enough to cycle around in about an hour. Rent a bicycle and pedal past coconut plantations, rubber trees, and quiet beaches. The west coast Ao Suan Yai beach has the best sunsets. Stop for a seafood lunch at a beachside restaurant, then continue to the southern tip for snorkeling off the rocks. The island has been designated a Low Carbon Destination by Thailand's government.
tips: Rent a bike from your resort — most include them free or charge 50-100 THB
The island is flat so cycling is easy for all fitness levels
Ao Suan Yai on the west coast has the best sunset views

activities.name: Snorkel at Koh Rang Marine Park
activities.description: Join a full-day snorkeling tour from Koh Chang to the uninhabited Koh Rang island group, home to some of the best coral reefs in the Gulf of Thailand. Visibility reaches 20+ meters in high season. Tours typically visit 3-4 snorkeling spots around Koh Rang, Koh Yak, and Koh Laoya. Expect to see colorful reef fish, sea anemones, and if lucky, sea turtles.
activities.price: 800-1,500 THB (~$22-42 USD) plus 200 THB park fee
tips: Book through dive shops on Koh Chang — they have the best boats and guides
Best visibility November to April
Bring your own snorkel mask if you have one — tour-provided ones can be low quality

activities.name: Explore Trat Old Town on Foot
activities.description: Wander the narrow lanes of Trat's historic center along the river, past century-old wooden shophouses, small Chinese shrines, and the Trat Museum. The old town retains its authentic charm with wooden buildings, local shops, and few tourists. The gem-trading heritage is visible in small jewelry shops that still operate along the main streets. End at the riverside area for atmospheric photos.
tips: The narrow lanes near the river have the most photogenic wooden houses
Visit in the late afternoon and transition into the night market
The Trat Museum is small but worth a quick look for local history
activities.bestTime: Late afternoon (cooler, leads into night market)

activities.name: Eat at Trat Night Market
activities.description: Trat's nightly food market on Tha Ruea Road is one of eastern Thailand's best — and one of the cheapest. Stalls sell grilled seafood, som tam, pad thai, curries over rice, fresh tropical fruit smoothies, and Thai desserts. Prices are genuine local rates (30-80 THB per dish) since this is a market for Trat residents, not tourists. Open every evening from around 5 PM.
tips: Try the grilled squid and prawn skewers — Trat's seafood is incredibly fresh
The market runs nightly but weekends are busiest
Arrive by 5:30 PM for the widest selection — popular stalls sell out early

activities.name: Visit Than Mayom Waterfall
activities.description: A historic waterfall on Koh Chang's quieter east coast, famous for royal visits. King Rama V and King Rama VII carved their initials into the rocks here, which are still visible near the second tier. The jungle trail to the three tiers is less crowded than Klong Plu, making it a more peaceful experience. The surrounding forest is dense and atmospheric.
tips: Look for the royal initials carved near the second tier
Use the same national park ticket from Klong Plu if visiting both in one day
The east coast road is less developed — fuel up before heading there

activities.name: Get PADI Certified on Koh Chang
activities.description: Several dive shops on Koh Chang offer PADI Open Water certification courses, typically over 3-4 days. Training dives take place at sheltered sites around Koh Chang and Koh Rang, with coral reefs, tropical fish, and occasional whale shark sightings in season. Koh Chang diving is less crowded and cheaper than popular Andaman coast dive sites.
activities.price: 12,000-15,000 THB (~$336-420 USD) for PADI Open Water
tips: Compare at least 3 dive shops — prices and group sizes vary
BB Divers and Scuba Dawgs are well-established operators
Best visibility November to April when seas are calmest

activities.name: Visit Bang Bao Fishing Village
activities.description: A picturesque fishing village at the southern tip of Koh Chang, built on stilts over the water and connected by a long wooden boardwalk. The boardwalk is lined with souvenir shops, restaurants, and dive operators. The pier at the end is the departure point for snorkeling tours to Koh Rang and ferries to Koh Mak and Koh Kood. Great for a sunset stroll with fresh seafood at the end.
tips: Walk to the end of the pier for the best views and departure point for island trips
The seafood restaurants at the pier are touristy but the setting is unique
Come at sunset for the most atmospheric visit

activities.name: Kayak Through Mangrove Forests
activities.description: Explore the mangrove ecosystems along Koh Chang's east coast or Trat's mainland by kayak. Guided tours paddle through narrow waterways shaded by mangrove canopy, spotting crabs, mudskippers, kingfishers, and monitor lizards. The Salak Phet mangrove area on Koh Chang's southeast coast is the most accessible. Some tours combine kayaking with a visit to a local fishing village.
tips: Wear long sleeves and bring insect repellent — mosquitoes are active in mangroves
Morning tours offer cooler temperatures and more wildlife activity
The Salak Phet area on Koh Chang is the best-organized for kayaking

activities.name: Taste Durian in Trat Province
activities.description: Trat is Thailand's durian capital, and the province's orchards produce some of the country's best fruit. During durian season (April-June), roadside stalls sell premium Monthong and Chanee varieties at half the Bangkok price. Many orchards offer tastings and tours where you can learn to pick ripe durian, taste different varieties, and buy direct from the grower. Even if you think you don't like durian, Trat's fresh-from-the-tree version may convert you.
tips: April to June is peak durian season — the rest of the year availability is limited
Ask for Monthong variety — it's creamier and less pungent than others
Roadside stalls along Route 3 between Trat and Khlong Yai have the freshest supply

activities.name: Sunset at Kai Bae Viewpoint
activities.description: One of Koh Chang's best viewpoints overlooking Kai Bae Beach and the offshore islands of Koh Man Nai. The viewpoint is along the main road between Kai Bae and Lonely Beach, with a small parking area. On clear days, you can see all the way to Koh Mak and Koh Kood on the horizon. Bring a drink and watch the sun sink into the Gulf of Thailand.
tips: Arrive 30 minutes before sunset for the best colors
The viewpoint is between Kai Bae and Lonely Beach on the main road — look for the small parking area
Combine with dinner at a Kai Bae beach restaurant

activities.name: Snorkel off Koh Wai
activities.description: A tiny, mostly undeveloped island between Koh Chang and Koh Mak with excellent snorkeling directly off the beach. The coral reefs start just meters from the shoreline, making it perfect for beginners. Most visitors come on day trips from Koh Chang, but there are a couple of basic bungalow operations for overnight stays. The island has no roads, no vehicles, and no nightlife — just beach and reef.
activities.price: Day trip from Koh Chang 800-1,200 THB (~$22-34 USD)
tips: Bring your own snorkel gear for the best experience — rentals on the island are basic
The beach on the north side has the best reef for snorkeling
Day trips typically include lunch at one of the island's two restaurants

activities.name: Thai Cooking Class on Koh Chang
activities.description: Several cooking schools on Koh Chang offer half-day and full-day classes where you learn to make 4-6 Thai dishes. Classes typically start with a market tour to buy fresh ingredients, then cook dishes like pad thai, green curry, tom yum soup, and mango sticky rice. You eat everything you cook. Most classes are on the west coast near White Sand Beach or Klong Prao.
tips: Book a class that includes a market visit — it adds context to the ingredients
Morning classes are more comfortable temperature-wise than afternoon
Ask about dietary accommodations — most can do vegetarian or seafood-only

activities.name: Explore Klong Chao Waterfall on Koh Kood
activities.description: Koh Kood's most famous attraction — a wide, gentle waterfall that flows into a large natural swimming pool surrounded by jungle. The water is clear enough to see the bottom, and the pool is deep enough for jumping from the rocks. A short trail from the road leads to the falls. After swimming, rent a kayak and paddle down the Klong Chao river through the jungle back to the coast.
tips: Combine with kayaking down the Klong Chao river to the sea
Visit during or just after rainy season (Jun-Oct) for the strongest water flow
The path from the road is short but can be muddy — wear appropriate shoes

activities.name: Visit Laem Ngop Pier & Monument
activities.description: The main departure point for ferries to the islands also holds historical significance — the Laem Ngop Naval Battle Monument commemorates a 1941 naval engagement between Thai and French forces. The pier area has a small museum, seafood restaurants, and views over the water to the islands. A good place to spend time while waiting for a ferry, or as a mainland day trip.
tips: Combine with catching a ferry to the islands
The seafood restaurants near the pier serve fresh local catch at good prices
The naval battle monument and museum give interesting historical context to the area

activities.name: Night Dive or Night Snorkel on Koh Chang
activities.description: Experience the underwater world after dark. Several dive operators on Koh Chang offer night dives at shallow sites where you can spot bioluminescent plankton, sleeping reef fish, hunting moray eels, and nocturnal creatures like octopus and squid. Night snorkeling trips are available for non-divers at some of the west coast beaches.
tips: You need at least an Open Water certification for night dives
Night snorkeling from the beach with a waterproof torch is a free alternative
Best conditions in calm season November to April

activities.name: Treetop Adventure Park on Koh Chang
activities.description: An adventure park in the jungle canopy near Klong Prao Beach with ziplines, rope bridges, and climbing challenges strung between old-growth trees. Multiple courses range from easy (suitable for children) to challenging with high ziplines over the jungle canopy. A fun half-day activity when you need a break from the beach.
tips: Book the morning session — it's cooler and less crowded
Wear closed-toe shoes and clothes you don't mind getting dirty
Children's courses available from age 4+

activities.name: Seafood BBQ on the Beach
activities.description: Many restaurants along Koh Chang's beaches set up evening BBQ spreads with the day's fresh catch displayed on ice. Choose your prawns, squid, fish, or crab and have them grilled over charcoal right on the sand. White Sand Beach and Kai Bae have the most beachfront BBQ options. Pair with a cold Singha beer and a Gulf of Thailand sunset.
tips: Seafood is priced by weight — always confirm the total before ordering
The smaller restaurants at Kai Bae tend to be better value than White Sand Beach
Fresh red snapper (pla krapong) grilled whole with salt crust is the local specialty
